mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal manufacturing contraction, which reduces corporate profit margins and forces negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage growth, which supports consumer spending and generates positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0% year-over-year growth rate, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand despite aggregate output growth, signaling an impending earnings slowdown and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-Retail Sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with forward returns remaining positive in more than 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicate persistent supply chain bottlenecks, which trigger immediate market pessimism and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not follow industrial production declines in post-shock environments, or the mechanism shows no statistical edge in walk-forward tests.
